acceptance_conditions.condition: protocol, selector, route identities, and all source hashes remain exact
12 adaptation groups produce 96 three-arm rows and 288 executions
all adapted and target-specific compiled routes retain semantic fidelity at least 0.9999999999
portfolio adapted-automatic mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower is at least -0.01
portfolio adapted-target mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower is at least -0.01
at least 11 of 12 groups have mean adapted-target delta at least -0.02
zero rows have adapted-target regression below -0.05
each target mean is at least -0.01 and Lagos dense-XY mean is at least -0.02 with zero severe rows
challenge commitment, hidden seeds, row hashes, reveal, and transcript replay
temporal, cross-machine, hardware, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

challenge_protocol.adaptation_rule: use the frozen R147 foreign route selected only by target readout/CX exposure descriptors and recompile on target
challenge_protocol.automatic_rule: fresh optimization-level-3 compile on target with a hidden transpiler seed
challenge_protocol.shared_seed_rule: all three arms in one row share one simulator seed
challenge_protocol.target_rule: recompile the target-specific R143 route on target only as a denominator

claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported_even_if_accepted: temporal same-device transfer, cross-machine transfer, real hardware, mitigation, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or solved B4/B8/B10

phase_protocol: commit a fresh secret after public preregistration
derive hidden transpiler, simulator, and bootstrap seeds
replay frozen adapted and target-specific route identities on each target
execute all three arms with one shared simulator seed per row
reveal and verify the acceptance transcript
research_question: Can a route selected only by target calibration descriptors repair R146 cross-snapshot failures without reading R146 trial rows or copying the target-specific route?
